Zero Dark Thirty (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $95,720,716Details
International Box Office $38,891,719
Worldwide Box Office $134,612,435
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,439,095
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,557,935
Total $14,997,030
Further financial details...

Synopsis

For a decade, an elite team of intelligence and military operatives, working in secret across the globe, devoted themselves to a single goal: to find and eliminate Osama bin Laden. A determined CIA agent follows her intuition to find him.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$417,150 (0.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.92 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:71.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$52,500,000 (worldwide box office is 2.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:5 opening theaters/2,946 max. theaters, 6.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $140,859,570

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 19th, 2012 (Exclusive) by Sony Pictures
January 11th, 2013 (Expands Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: January 4th, 2013 (Wide) (Spain)
January 11th, 2013 (Wide) (Philippines)
January 11th, 2013 (Wide) (Taiwan)
January 17th, 2013 (Wide) (Croatia)
January 17th, 2013 (Wide) (Portugal)
Video Release: March 19th, 2013 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong violence including brutal disturbing images, and for language.
(Rating bulletin 2250, 11/21/2012)
Running Time: 137 minutes
Award nomination: 2013 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Plot point: 9/11, C.I.A., Double Agent, Secret Agent, Special Ops, Suicide Bomber, Terrorism, Tortured for Information
Social setting: Afghanistan War
Subgenre: Political Thriller
Time period setting: 2000s, 2010s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Mark Boal, First Light, Annapurna Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

For a decade, an elite team of intelligence and military operatives, working in secret across the globe, devoted themselves to a single goal: to find and eliminate Osama bin Laden. A determined CIA agent follows her intuition to find him.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$417,150 (0.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.92 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:71.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$52,500,000 (worldwide box office is 2.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:5 opening theaters/2,946 max. theaters, 6.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $140,859,570

Domestic Releases: December 19th, 2012 (Exclusive) by Sony Pictures
January 11th, 2013 (Expands Wide) by Sony Pictures

January 11th, 2013 (Wide) (Philippines)
January 11th, 2013 (Wide) (Taiwan)
January 17th, 2013 (Wide) (Croatia)
January 17th, 2013 (Wide) (Portugal)
Video Release: March 19th, 2013 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong violence including brutal disturbing images, and for language.
(Rating bulletin 2250, 11/21/2012)
Running Time: 137 minutes
Award nomination: 2013 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
